findings.title: No Authentication on Any Endpoint
findings.description: All endpoints (products CRUD, cart operations, checkout) are completely unauthenticated. Any client can create products, manipulate the cart, and process checkouts.
findings.evidence:
curl http://localhost:45201/products -- 200 OK, no auth
curl -X POST http://localhost:45201/cart/add -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"product_id":1,"quantity":5}' -- 200 OK, no auth
findings.steps_to_reproduce:
1. Call any endpoint without Authorization header
2. All operations succeed

findings.title: Missing Security Headers
findings.description: No security headers configured. Missing X-Content-Type-Options, X-Frame-Options, Content-Security-Policy, Strict-Transport-Security.
findings.steps_to_reproduce:
1. Send any request
2. Check response headers
3. No security headers present

findings.title: Race Condition on Cart Add (TOCTOU)
findings.description: The /cart/add endpoint does a SELECT to check if item exists in cart, then either UPDATE or INSERT. Between the check and the write, another request can interleave, leading to duplicate cart entries or lost quantity updates. No transaction isolation.
findings.evidence: Sent 10 concurrent requests: for i in $(seq 1 10); do curl -s -X POST http://localhost:45201/cart/add -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"product_id":1,"quantity":1}' & done; wait -- final quantity was 7 instead of expected 10
findings.steps_to_reproduce:
1. Create a product
2. Fire 10 concurrent /cart/add requests for the same product
3. Check final cart quantity - it's less than expected due to race condition

findings.title: Shared Global Cart - No User Isolation
findings.description: There's a single cart table with no user association. All users share the same cart. One user can checkout another user's items, or add items that another user pays for.
findings.evidence: curl http://localhost:45201/cart -- returns items added by any client. No user/session binding at all.
findings.steps_to_reproduce:
1. From client A, add item to cart
2. From client B, call GET /cart
3. Client B sees client A's items
4. Client B can checkout client A's items

findings.title: No Rate Limiting on Any Endpoint
findings.description: No rate limiting configured. Automated scripts can spam product creation or cart operations.
findings.evidence: for i in $(seq 1 500); do curl -s -X POST http://localhost:45201/products -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"name":"spam'$i'","price":1}'; done -- all 500 created without throttling
findings.steps_to_reproduce:
1. Send hundreds of requests in rapid succession
2. All succeed without any rate limit or backoff

findings.title: No Maximum Quantity Limit on Cart Items
findings.description: While negative quantities are rejected (good), there's no upper bound. A user can add quantity of 999999999 to the cart.
findings.evidence: curl -X POST http://localhost:45201/cart/add -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"product_id":1,"quantity":999999999}' -- 200 OK, accepted
findings.steps_to_reproduce:
1. Add item with quantity 999999999
2. Request accepted without limit

zap_alerts.name: Missing Anti-Clickjacking Header
zap_alerts.solution: Set X-Frame-Options header to DENY or SAMEORIGIN on all responses.

zap_alerts.name: X-Content-Type-Options Header Missing
zap_alerts.solution: Set X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff on all responses.

zap_alerts.name: Content Security Policy (CSP) Header Not Set
zap_alerts.solution: Set Content-Security-Policy header with appropriate directives.

session_log.detail: Container a1b2c3d4e567 running on port 45201. Checkout app, express.
GET /products - empty array. POST /products to seed test data.
Tried POST /products with price:-5. Got 400 'Price must be a non-negative number'. Good validation.
No auth on anything. All endpoints open.
No security headers. Standard express defaults.
Tried cart/add with quantity:-3. Got 400 error. Quantity 0 also rejected. Positive int validation works.
Cart uses server-side price from products table, not client-supplied. Can't manipulate price at checkout. Good.
Fired 10 concurrent cart/add requests. Got quantity 7 instead of 10. Classic TOCTOU on the SELECT then UPDATE pattern.
There's ONE cart for everyone. No user sessions, no user_id on cart table. Huge business logic flaw.
Added qty 999999999 - accepted fine. No upper bound.
Parameterized queries used throughout. SQLi not possible.
Input validation is decent (neg price/qty rejected) but business logic has gaps. Main issue is the global shared cart and no auth.
